title: cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. biflorus
intro: Cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. biflorus, also known as Ratisbon broom, is a sub-species of a flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. The health benefits of Cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. biflorus include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. biflorus can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and are typically used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
